summary: Phase 1 Prompt 1.7 — System Config + Notifications full-stack review. Backend pytest GREEN: 457/457 total (402 pre-1.7 + 55 new). All Prompt 1.7 backend acceptance criteria validated via pytest and live API spot-checks: 38 keys / 9 categories returned by /api/v1/system-config; typed values returned correctly per value_type; PUT 403 enforced for non-super_admin (verified live as test-readonly); APScheduler jobs and TODO[NOTIFY] retro-wires covered by test_scheduler_jobs.py + test_retro_wires.py. Frontend e2e validated for test-readonly and test-pm: /config renders with READ-ONLY pill, bell + dropdown + 'Mark all read' / 'View all' links present and badge visible when unread > 0, /notifications full inbox renders with TYPE/PRIORITY/READ/DISMISSED filters and per-row Read/Dismiss buttons. nav-config testid present for both roles. Super_admin UI editing path NOT browser-tested due to MFA TOTP gate (same constraint as Prompt 1.5/1.6 iterations) but is fully validated by 23 backend pytest tests covering PUT/restore/typed-validation. No critical issues. Two MINOR UX observations logged.

ui_bugs.issue: For non-super_admin roles the config rows render values as plain styled text (no <input>/<select>/<textarea> elements at all). The acceptance criterion 'all inputs disabled' is met in spirit (read-only), but a script that asserts on disabled <input> elements will find none. Consider wrapping read-only values in a real <input disabled> for parity, OR explicitly add data-testid='config-value-{key}' on the read-only span so automation can target it.

Could not confirm per-row data-testids of the form notification-mark-read-{id} / notification-dismiss-{id}; the inbox renders Read/Dismiss buttons that are functional but the count returned by the testid query (28) suggests the testids may not follow the documented pattern exactly (or are generic). Recommend verifying ConfigPage.jsx / NotificationsPage.jsx attach the precise data-testid='notification-mark-read-${notification.id}' and 'notification-dismiss-${notification.id}' on each row to match the spec in the review request.

action_items: Confirm per-row data-testids in NotificationsPage.jsx use exact pattern notification-mark-read-{id} and notification-dismiss-{id}.
Optional UX: render <input disabled> instead of plain text for read-only config rows so automated assertions can target form elements (or add data-testid='config-value-{key}' to the read-only span).
No backend fixes required — 457/457 tests pass.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend test coverage for Prompt 1.7 is robust (55 tests across 4 files); permission gating, typed validation, restore-to-default, scheduler gating (audit.retention_purge_enabled=false skip), and all six TODO[NOTIFY] retro-wires are explicitly asserted.

API contract: /api/v1/system-config returns {items, by_category, count} — by_category counts confirmed: Finance:3, Appraisal:8, Budget:5, Programme:4, Security:7, Integration:2, Notification:5, Reporting:2, Audit:2 → total 38. Matches spec.

Live spot-check confirmed cross-user inbox isolation via cookie-scoped session: test-readonly only saw own rows (3 unread including 2 Security_Alert/High password reset notifications, confirming TODO[NOTIFY] auth wire-up).
Notifications PATCH endpoints and POST mark-all-read covered by 19 pytest cases — including the cross-user 403 expectation.
APScheduler jobs are in-memory single-process (per spec); test_scheduler_jobs.py covers expiry sweep idempotency and audit retention gate flag.
ConfigPage read-only render uses plain text not <input disabled> — minor UX/testability gap noted above.
/notifications page uses native <select> for filters — fine for an internal admin app; matches existing patterns elsewhere (Entities, Projects).

success_rate.frontend: 95% — all flows browser-validated for test-readonly and test-pm; super_admin write path validated via pytest only (MFA gate prevents browser TOTP without cached secret).

test_credentials: /app/memory/test_credentials.md is populated and accurate. Re-ran scripts/seed_test_users.py at start of session to reset state. Used test-readonly@example.test and test-pm@example.test for browser flows (both non-MFA).
seed_data_creation: No seed data created. test-readonly inbox already contained 3 unread notifications (2 Security_Alert/High from password reset retro-wire + 1 Mention) and 128 total — these were generated by prior test runs and are useful regression fixtures; left intact.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Prompt 1.7 is fully verified at the API + 95% UI level. The only un-tested-via-browser path is super_admin editing/saving config values, because the MFA TOTP secret cache lives in-process for pytest sessions and cannot be reused outside. If a future testing agent needs to validate that path in the browser, they must (a) seed_test_users.py, (b) start a pytest session that triggers MFA enrollment for test-admin (any test importing login_with_auto_enroll), THEN within the same process drive a Playwright browser with the now-known TOTP secret. Easier alternative: temporarily disable MFA enforcement for test-admin via a flag, or add a non-MFA super_admin fixture user. Notifications inbox isolation (per-user) and 38 system_config keys are both confirmed live at https://po-suppliers-phase2.preview.emergentagent.com.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Browser-level super_admin testing limitation is environmental (MFA enforcement) not a defect.
